name: Oven-Baked Bourguignonne
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Medium Or Broad Egg Noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut bonless chuck into 1-inch cubes as with stew meat.
2. Peel and chop tomatoes, using whole peeled tomatoes that are undrained.
3. Choose either onion or beefy onion soup mix.
4. Slice mushrooms; use 1 cup of sliced mushrooms or slice up 8 oz fresh mushrooms.
Preheat Oven to 400 degrees F.
5. Toss beef with flour in a 2-quart casserole and bake uncovered for 20 minutes.
6. Add carrots, tomatoes, and bay leaf, then add the chosen soup mix blended with wine.
7. Bake covered for 1 1/2 hours or until beef is tender.
8. Add mushrooms and bake covered for an additional 10 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
Meanwhile:
9. Cook noodles according to package directions.
Serve:
10. Arrange bourguignonne over noodles.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
11. Toss beef with flour, set aside.
12. In the same 2-quart casserole, combine tomatoes, bay leaf, and chosen soup mix blended with wine. Heat covered at HIGH (Full Power) for 7 minutes, stirring once.
13. Add beef and carrots. Heat covered at DEFROST (30% FULL POWER), stirring occasionally, for 1 1/4 hours.
14. Add mushrooms and heat covered at DEFROST (30% FULL POWER) for an additional 30 minutes or until beef is tender. Remove bay leaf. Let stand covered for 5 minutes.
FREEZING/REHEATING DIRECTIONS:
15. Bake the bourguignonne, then freeze by wrapping it covered in heavy-duty aluminum foil.
16. To reheat, unwrap and bake covered at 400 degrees F., stirring occasionally to separate beef and vegetables, for 1 hour. OR, microwave at HIGH (Full Power), stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes or until heated through. Let stand covered for 5 minutes.
